title: Leviticus 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD called to Moses. He spoke to him from the tent of meeting. He said:
2 "Speak to the Israelites. Tell them: When any of you brings an offering to the LORD, you may bring an animal from the herd or the flock.
3 If your burnt offering is from the herd, bring a bull without defect. Bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting. Then it will be accepted before the LORD.
4 Lay your hand on its head. Then it will be accepted for you to make atonement for you.
5 Slaughter the young bull before the LORD. Then Aaron's sons, the priests, will bring the blood. They will sprinkle the blood on all sides of the altar at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
6 Next, skin the burnt offering and cut it into pieces.
7 The sons of Aaron the priest will put fire on the altar. They will arrange wood on the fire.
8 Then Aaron's sons, the priests, will arrange the pieces. They will place the head and the fat on the burning wood on the altar.
9 Wash the inside parts and the legs with water. Then the priest will burn all of it on the altar. It is a burnt offering, a food offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
10 If your burnt offering is from the flock — from the sheep or the goats — bring a male without defect.
11 Slaughter it on the north side of the altar before the LORD. Then Aaron's sons, the priests, will sprinkle its blood on all sides of the altar.
12 Cut the animal into pieces. The priest will arrange them, with the head and the fat, on the burning wood on the altar.
13 Wash the inside parts and the legs with water. Then the priest will bring all of it and burn it on the altar. It is a burnt offering, a food offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
14 If your burnt offering to the LORD is birds, bring a turtledove or a young pigeon.
15 The priest will bring it to the altar. He will twist off its head and burn it on the altar. He will drain its blood out on the side of the altar.
16 He will remove the throat sac with what is in it. He will throw it to the east side of the altar, in the place for ashes.

17 He will tear it open by the wings, but not split the bird in two. Then the priest will burn it on the altar, on the burning wood. It is a burnt offering, a food offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD."

title: Leviticus 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 "When anyone brings a grain offering to the LORD, the offering must be made of fine flour. He must pour olive oil on it and put frankincense on it.

2 Then he must bring it to Aaron's sons, the priests. The priest will take a handful of the flour and oil, with all the frankincense. He will burn this as a memorial portion on the altar. It is a food offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
3 The rest of the grain offering will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is a most holy part of the food offerings made by fire to the LORD.
4 "If you bring a grain offering baked in an oven, it must be made of fine flour. It can be unleavened cakes mixed with oil. Or it can be unleavened thin cakes brushed with oil.
5 If your grain offering is cooked on a griddle, it must be unleavened bread. It must be made of fine flour mixed with oil.
6 Break it into pieces and pour oil on it. It is a grain offering.
7 If your grain offering is cooked in a pan, it must be made of fine flour with oil.
8 When you bring to the LORD a grain offering made in any of these ways, give it to the priest. He will take it to the altar.
9 The priest will take out the memorial portion from the grain offering. He will burn it on the altar as a food offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
10 But the rest of the grain offering will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is a most holy part of the food offerings made by fire to the LORD.
11 "Every grain offering you bring to the LORD must be made without yeast. You must not burn any yeast or honey in a food offering to the LORD.
12 You may bring them to the LORD as an offering of firstfruits. But you must not offer them on the altar as a pleasing aroma.
13 Add salt to all your grain offerings. Do not leave the salt of the covenant of your God out of your grain offerings. Add salt to all your offerings.
14 "If you bring a grain offering of firstfruits to the LORD, offer crushed heads of new grain roasted in fire.
15 Put oil and frankincense on it. It is a grain offering.
16 The priest will then burn the memorial portion of the crushed grain and the oil, with all its frankincense. It is a food offering made by fire to the LORD."

title: Leviticus 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 "If a person's offering is a peace offering from the herd, he must bring it. The animal may be male or female. It must be without defect before the LORD.
2 He is to place his hand on the head of the offering. Then he must kill it at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. Aaron's sons the priests will splash the blood against all sides of the altar.
3 From the peace offering he must bring a food offering to the LORD. This includes the fat that covers the inner parts. It also includes all the fat on them,
4 the two kidneys with the fat on them near the lower back, and the long lobe of the liver. He must remove these with the kidneys.
5 Then Aaron's sons will burn it on the altar. It goes on top of the burnt offering on the burning wood. It is a food offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
6 If a person's peace offering to the LORD is from the flock, he must bring a male or female without defect.
7 If he brings a lamb as his offering, he must bring it before the LORD.
8 He is to place his hand on the head of his offering. Then he must kill it in front of the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron's sons will splash its blood against all sides of the altar.

9 From the peace offering he must bring a food offering to the LORD. It will include the fat: the whole fat tail, cut off close to the backbone. It will also include the fat that covers the inner parts, and all the fat on them,
10 the two kidneys with the fat on them near the lower back, and the long lobe of the liver. He must remove these with the kidneys.
11 Then the priest will burn them on the altar as food. It is a food offering to the LORD.
12 If a person's offering is a goat, he is to bring it before the LORD.
13 He must place his hand on its head. Then he must kill it in front of the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron's sons will splash its blood against all sides of the altar.
14 From his offering he must bring a food offering to the LORD: the fat that covers the inner parts, and all the fat on them,
15 the two kidneys with the fat on them near the lower back, and the long lobe of the liver. He must remove these with the kidneys.
16 Then the priest will burn the food on the altar. It is a food offering, a pleasing aroma. All the fat belongs to the LORD.
17 This is a lasting ordinance for the generations to come, wherever you live: You must not eat any fat or any blood."

title: Leviticus 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Tell the Israelites this. Someone may sin by mistake against any of the LORD's commands. He may do what is not allowed. Then follow these rules.
3 If the anointed priest sins, he brings guilt on the people. He must bring a young bull with no defect to the LORD. It is a sin offering for the sin he did.
4 He must bring the bull to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He brings it before the LORD. He lays his hand on the bull's head. Then he kills it before the LORD.
5 Then the anointed priest takes some of the bull's blood. He brings it into the Tent of Meeting.
6 The priest dips his finger in the blood. He sprinkles some of it seven times before the LORD. He sprinkles it in front of the curtain of the sanctuary.

7 The priest then puts some of the blood on the horns of the altar of sweet-smelling incense. This altar stands before the LORD in the Tent of Meeting. He pours out the rest of the bull's blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering. That altar is at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
8 Then he removes all the fat from the bull of the sin offering. He removes the fat that covers the inside parts and all the fat on them.
9 He also removes both kidneys with the fat near the loins. He removes the lobe of the liver with the kidneys.
10 He does this just as the fat is removed from the ox of the peace offering. Then the priest burns them on the altar of burnt offering.
11 But the hide of the bull and all its flesh remain. So do its head, its legs, its inside parts, and its dung.
12 The priest takes all the rest of the bull outside the camp. He brings it to a clean place where the ashes are thrown. There he burns it on a wood fire on the ash heap.
13 Now the whole community of Israel may sin by mistake. The matter may be hidden from the assembly. They may break any of the LORD's commands and become guilty.
14 When they learn about the sin they did, the assembly must act. They must bring a young bull as a sin offering. They bring it before the Tent of Meeting.
15 The elders of the congregation lay their hands on the bull's head before the LORD. Then it is killed before the LORD.
16 Then the anointed priest brings some of the bull's blood into the Tent of Meeting.
17 He dips his finger in the blood. He sprinkles it seven times before the LORD in front of the curtain.

18 He also puts some of the blood on the horns of the altar before the LORD in the Tent of Meeting. He pours out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering. That altar is at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
19 He removes all the fat from it. Then he burns it on the altar.
20 He offers this bull just as he did the bull for the sin offering. In this way the priest will make atonement for them, and they will be forgiven.
21 Then he takes the bull outside the camp. He burns it, just as he burned the first bull. It is the sin offering for the assembly.
22 A leader may sin by mistake. He may do what is not allowed by any of the LORD his God's commands. So he becomes guilty.
23 When he learns about the sin he did, he must bring his offering. He must bring a male goat with no defect.
24 He lays his hand on the head of the goat. Then he kills it at the place where the burnt offering is killed before the LORD. It is a sin offering.
25 Then the priest takes some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger. He puts it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering. He pours out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
26 He burns all its fat on the altar, like the fat of the peace offerings. In this way the priest will make atonement for that man's sin, and he will be forgiven.
27 One of the common people may sin by mistake. He may do what is not allowed by any of the LORD's commands. So he becomes guilty.
28 When he learns about the sin he did, he must bring his offering. He must bring a female goat with no defect for that sin.
29 He lays his hand on the head of the sin offering. Then he kills it at the place of the burnt offering.
30 Then the priest takes some of its blood with his finger. He puts it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering. He pours out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.

31 Then he removes all the fat, just as it is removed from the peace offering. The priest burns it on the altar as a sweet smell pleasing to the LORD. In this way the priest will make atonement for him, and he will be forgiven.
32 But if he brings a lamb as a sin offering, he must bring a female with no defect.
33 He lays his hand on the head of the sin offering. Then he kills it as a sin offering at the place where the burnt offering is killed.
34 Then the priest takes some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger. He puts it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering. He pours out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.

35 He removes all the fat, just as the fat of the lamb is removed from the peace offerings. He burns it on the altar along with the offerings made by fire to the LORD. In this way the priest will make atonement for him for the sin he did, and he will be forgiven.

title: Leviticus 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 "Someone may sin in this way. He hears a public call to give witness. He has seen or learned something. But he refuses to speak. Then he must carry the guilt.
2 Or a person may touch something unclean. It may be the dead body of a wild animal, a farm animal, or a small animal that moves on the ground. Even if he did not know, he is unclean and guilty.
3 Or he may touch human uncleanness — anything that makes a person unclean. Even if he did not know it, he is guilty when he becomes aware of it.
4 Or someone may make a thoughtless oath with his lips. He may swear to do good or evil — anything a person might say in a rush. Even if he did not know it, he is guilty when he becomes aware of it.
5 When anyone becomes aware of their sin in any of these ways, he must confess the way in which he has sinned.

6 Then he must bring his guilt offering to the LORD for the sin he has done. He must bring a female lamb or goat from the flock as a sin offering. And the priest will make atonement for him for his sin.
7 If he cannot afford a lamb, he may bring two doves or two pigeons to the LORD. One will be a sin offering, and the other will be a burnt offering.
8 He must bring them to the priest. The priest will offer the first one as the sin offering. He will twist its head at the front of its neck, but not pull it off.
9 Then he will sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar. He will pour the rest of the blood at the base of the altar. It is a sin offering.
10 The priest will prepare the second bird as a burnt offering, in the right way. In this way, the priest will make atonement for him for the sin he has done. And he will be forgiven.
11 But if he cannot afford two doves or two pigeons, he may bring a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a sin offering. He must not put olive oil or incense on it, because it is a sin offering.
12 He must bring it to the priest. The priest will take a handful as a special part to remember. He will burn it on the altar on top of the offerings made by fire to the LORD. It is a sin offering.
13 In this way, the priest will make atonement for him for any of these sins he has done. And he will be forgiven. The rest will belong to the priest, like the grain offering."
14 Then the LORD said to Moses,

15 "Someone may act in a wrong way. He may sin without meaning to against any of the LORD's holy things. Then he must bring his guilt offering to the LORD. It must be a ram with no defect from the flock. Its value must be set in silver shekels by the sanctuary shekel. It is a guilt offering (asham).

16 He must make restitution for the holy thing he has harmed. He must add a fifth of its value and give it to the priest. The priest will make atonement for him with the ram as a guilt offering. And he will be forgiven.
17 Someone may sin and break any of the LORD's commands without knowing it. He is still guilty and must carry his punishment.

18 He must bring a ram with no defect from the flock to the priest. Its value must be set as a guilt offering. Then the priest will make atonement for him for the wrong he has done without knowing. And he will be forgiven.
19 It is a guilt offering. He was truly guilty before the LORD."

title: Leviticus 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Someone may sin and do wrong against the LORD. He may cheat his neighbor about money or things put in his care. He may also take something by force.
3 Or he may find lost property and lie about it. He may lie under an oath. Or he may do any sin like these —
4 once he has sinned and is guilty, he must give back what he took. He must return what he stole or took by force. He must return what was put in his care, or the lost property he found.
5 He must return anything he lied about under oath. He must pay back the full amount. He must add one-fifth more. He must pay it to the owner on the day he admits his guilt.
6 Then he must bring his guilt offering to the priest. It must be a ram from the flock, with no defect, of proper value.
7 In this way the priest will make atonement for him before the LORD. Then the LORD will forgive him for anything that made him guilty."
8 Then the LORD said to Moses,

9 "Give this command to Aaron and his sons. This is the law of the burnt offering. The burnt offering must stay on the fire-place of the altar all night, until morning. The fire on the altar must be kept burning.
10 The priest shall put on his linen robe and linen underclothes. He shall remove the ashes of the burnt offering from the altar. He shall place them beside the altar.
11 Then he must take off these clothes and put on other clothes. He must carry the ashes outside the camp to a clean place.

12 The fire on the altar must be kept burning. It must not go out. Every morning the priest shall add wood to the fire. He shall arrange the burnt offering on it. He shall burn the fat of the peace offerings on it.
13 The fire must be kept burning on the altar continually. It must not go out.
14 Now this is the law of the grain offering. Aaron's sons shall present it before the LORD in front of the altar.

15 The priest shall take a handful of fine flour and olive oil. He shall also take all the frankincense from the grain offering. He shall burn this memorial portion on the altar. It is a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
16 Aaron and his sons shall eat what is left. They must eat it without yeast in a holy place. They shall eat it in the courtyard of the Tent of Meeting.
17 It must not be baked with yeast. I have given it to them as their share of my offerings made by fire. It is most holy, like the sin offering and the guilt offering.

18 Any male among Aaron's sons may eat it. This is their permanent share of the offerings made by fire to the LORD. It is for all the generations to come. Anything that touches them will become holy."
19 Then the LORD said to Moses,

20 "This is the offering that Aaron and his sons must bring to the LORD. They must bring it on the day he is anointed. It is a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering. Half of it is for the morning and half for the evening.
21 It shall be prepared with oil on a flat pan. Bring it well-mixed. Present it as a grain offering, broken in pieces. It is a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
22 The priest who is anointed to take Aaron's place shall prepare it. He is one of Aaron's sons. This is a permanent share for the LORD. It must be burned completely.
23 Every grain offering for a priest must be burned completely. It must not be eaten."
24 And the LORD said to Moses,
25 "Tell Aaron and his sons. This is the law of the sin offering. The sin offering shall be killed before the LORD. It must be killed in the place where the burnt offering is killed. It is most holy.
26 The priest who offers it shall eat it. It must be eaten in a holy place, in the courtyard of the Tent of Meeting.
27 Anything that touches its meat will become holy. If any of the blood splashes on clothes, you must wash them in a holy place.
28 The clay pot used to boil the sin offering must be broken. If it is boiled in a bronze pot, the pot must be scrubbed and washed with water.
29 Any male among the priests may eat it. It is most holy.
30 But no sin offering may be eaten if its blood has been brought into the Tent of Meeting. The blood is brought there to make atonement in the Holy Place. Such an offering must be burned."

title: Leviticus 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 "Now this is the law of the guilt offering. It is most holy.
2 The guilt offering must be killed at the same place as the burnt offering. Then the priest shall sprinkle its blood on all sides of the altar.
3 All the fat from it shall be offered. This includes the fat tail and the fat that covers the inside parts.
4 It also includes both kidneys with the fat near the lower back. The priest shall remove the best part of the liver with the kidneys.
5 The priest shall burn them on the altar. It is an offering made by fire to the LORD. It is a guilt offering.
6 Every male among the priests may eat of it. They must eat it in a holy place. It is most holy.
7 The guilt offering is like the sin offering. The same law applies to both. It belongs to the priest who makes atonement with it.
8 A priest may present a burnt offering for someone. The skin of that offering belongs to him.
9 Every grain offering baked in an oven belongs to the priest who offers it. So does every offering cooked in a pan or on a flat pan.
10 Every other grain offering, dry or mixed with oil, belongs to all the sons of Aaron equally.
11 Now this is the law of the peace offering. A person may present it to the LORD.

12 Someone may offer it as a thanksgiving offering. Then he must also offer unleavened cakes mixed with olive oil. He must offer unleavened wafers spread with oil. He must offer well-mixed cakes of fine flour mixed with oil.
13 Along with his thanksgiving peace offering, he must also present cakes of leavened bread.
14 From these cakes, he must give one of each as a contribution to the LORD. It belongs to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the peace offering.
15 He must eat the meat of his thanksgiving peace offering on the day he offers it. He must not leave any of it until morning.
16 But the offering may be a vow offering or a freewill offering. Then he may eat it on the day he offers it. He may also eat the rest on the next day.
17 But any meat left until the third day must be burned up.

18 Someone may eat meat from his peace offering on the third day. Then God will not accept it. It will not count for the one who offered it. It is something God hates. The one who eats it must carry the guilt of his sin.
19 Any meat that touches anything unclean must not be eaten. It must be burned up. As for other meat, anyone who is clean may eat it.
20 A person may be unclean and eat meat from the peace offering that belongs to the LORD. That person must be cut off from his people.

21 A person may touch something unclean. It could be human uncleanness, an unclean animal, or any unclean and hated thing. Then he eats meat of the peace offering that belongs to the LORD. That person must be cut off from his people."
22 Then the LORD said to Moses,
23 "Speak to the Israelites and say: You must not eat any of the fat of an ox, a sheep, or a goat.
24 The fat of an animal found dead or killed by wild animals may be used for other things. But you must not eat it.
25 Someone may eat the fat of an animal that was offered by fire to the LORD. That person must be cut off from his people.
26 Wherever you live, you must not eat the blood of any bird or animal.
27 Anyone who eats blood must be cut off from his people."
28 Then the LORD said to Moses,
29 "Speak to the Israelites and say: Anyone who presents a peace offering to the LORD must bring it himself.
30 With his own hands he must bring the offerings made by fire to the LORD. He must bring the fat with the breast. He must wave the breast as a wave offering before the LORD.
31 The priest is to burn the fat on the altar. But the breast belongs to Aaron and his sons.
32 You must give the right thigh to the priest as a contribution from your peace offering.
33 The son of Aaron who offers the blood and fat of the peace offering shall receive the right thigh as his share.

34 I have taken the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution from the Israelites. These come from their peace offerings. I have given them to Aaron the priest and his sons. This is a share that lasts forever from the Israelites."
35 This is the share for Aaron and his sons from the offerings made by fire to the LORD. It began the day they were presented to serve the LORD as priests.
36 On the day they were anointed, the LORD commanded the Israelites to give this share to them. It is a share that lasts forever for all generations.
37 This is the law of the burnt offering, the grain offering, the sin offering, the guilt offering, the ordination offering, and the peace offering.
38 The LORD gave this law to Moses on Mount Sinai. He gave it on the day He commanded the Israelites to present their offerings to the LORD in the Sinai Desert.

title: Leviticus 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Take Aaron and his sons. Take their clothes, the anointing oil, and the bull for the sin offering. Take the two rams and the basket of unleavened bread.
3 Then gather all the people at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting."
4 So Moses did as the LORD had told him. The people gathered at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
5 Moses said to them, "This is what the LORD has commanded to be done."
6 Then Moses brought Aaron and his sons forward. He washed them with water.
7 He put the tunic on Aaron and tied the sash around him. He put the robe on him and then the ephod. He tied the woven band of the ephod around him.
8 Then he put the breastpiece on Aaron. He placed the Urim and Thummim in the breastpiece.
9 Moses put the turban on Aaron's head. He set the gold plate, the holy crown, on the front of the turban. He did this just as the LORD had told him.
10 Next, Moses took the anointing oil. He anointed the tabernacle and everything in it. So he set them apart as holy.
11 He sprinkled some of the oil on the altar seven times. He anointed the altar and all its tools. He also anointed the washing basin with its stand. So he set them apart as holy.
12 He poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head. He anointed him to set him apart as holy.
13 Then Moses brought Aaron's sons forward. He put tunics on them and wrapped sashes around them. He tied headbands on them. He did this just as the LORD had told him.
14 Moses then brought the bull for the sin offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.

15 Moses killed the bull. He took some of the blood with his finger. He put it on the four horns of the altar to purify it. He poured the rest of the blood at the base of the altar. So he set it apart as holy and made atonement on it.
16 Moses took all the fat from the inner parts. He took the lobe of the liver and both kidneys with their fat. He burned it all on the altar.
17 But he burned the bull's hide, flesh, and dung outside the camp. He did this just as the LORD had told him.
18 Then Moses brought the ram for the burnt offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.
19 Moses killed the ram. He sprinkled the blood on all sides of the altar.
20 He cut the ram into pieces. He burned the head, the pieces, and the fat.

21 He washed the inner parts and the legs with water. He burned the whole ram on the altar as a burnt offering. It was a pleasing smell, an offering made by fire to the LORD. He did this just as the LORD had told Moses.
22 After that, Moses brought the other ram, the ordination ram. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.
23 Moses killed the ram. He took some of its blood. He put it on Aaron's right earlobe, on his right thumb, and on his right big toe.

24 Moses also brought Aaron's sons forward. He put some of the blood on their right earlobes, on their right thumbs, and on their right big toes. Then he sprinkled the blood on all sides of the altar.
25 Moses took the fat — the fat tail, all the fat on the inner parts, the lobe of the liver, and both kidneys with their fat. He also took the right thigh.

26 From the basket of unleavened bread before the LORD, he took three things. He took one cake of unleavened bread, one cake of bread made with oil, and one wafer. He placed them on the fat and on the right thigh.
27 He put all these in the hands of Aaron and his sons. They waved them before the LORD as a wave offering.
28 Then Moses took them back from their hands. He burned them on the altar with the burnt offering. This was the ordination offering. It was a pleasing smell, an offering made by fire to the LORD.
29 Moses also took the breast and waved it before the LORD as a wave offering. This was his own share of the ordination ram. He did this just as the LORD had told him.

30 Next, Moses took some of the anointing oil. He also took some of the blood from the altar. He sprinkled them on Aaron and his clothes. He also sprinkled them on Aaron's sons and their clothes. So he set Aaron and his clothes apart as holy. He set Aaron's sons and their clothes apart too.

31 Moses said to Aaron and his sons, "Boil the meat at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Eat it there with the bread in the basket of ordination offerings. I have commanded, 'Aaron and his sons are to eat it.'
32 Then you must burn up the rest of the meat and bread.
33 You must not go outside the entrance of the Tent of Meeting for seven days. Stay there until the days of your ordination are over. It will take seven days to ordain you.
34 The LORD has commanded what has been done today. This is to make atonement for you.
35 You must stay at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting day and night for seven days. You must keep the LORD's charge so that you will not die. This is what I have been commanded."
36 So Aaron and his sons did everything the LORD had commanded through Moses.

title: Leviticus 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 On the eighth day, Moses called Aaron and his sons. He also called the elders of Israel.
2 He said to Aaron, "Take a young bull for a sin offering. Take a ram for a burnt offering. Both must have no defects. Bring them before the LORD.
3 Then speak to the Israelites. Say, 'Take a male goat for a sin offering. Take a calf and a lamb, both one year old and with no defects, for a burnt offering.
4 Take an ox and a ram for a peace offering before the LORD. Also take a grain offering mixed with oil. For today the LORD will appear to you.'"
5 So they took what Moses had commanded to the front of the tent of meeting. All the people came close and stood before the LORD.
6 Moses said, "This is what the LORD has commanded you to do. Then the glory of the LORD will appear to you."

7 Then Moses said to Aaron, "Go to the altar. Offer your sin offering and your burnt offering. Make atonement for yourself and for the people. Then offer the people's offering to make atonement for them, as the LORD has commanded."
8 So Aaron went to the altar. He killed the calf as a sin offering for himself.
9 Aaron's sons brought the blood to him. He dipped his finger in the blood and put it on the horns of the altar. Then he poured the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
10 On the altar he burned the fat, the kidneys, and part of the liver from the sin offering. He did this as the LORD had commanded Moses.
11 But he burned up the flesh and the skin outside the camp.
12 Then Aaron killed the burnt offering. His sons brought him the blood. He sprinkled it on all sides of the altar.
13 They brought him the burnt offering piece by piece, with the head. He burned them on the altar.
14 He washed the inside parts and the legs. Then he burned them on top of the burnt offering on the altar.
15 Next, Aaron presented the people's offering. He took the male goat for the people's sin offering. He killed it and offered it for sin, just like the first one.
16 He presented the burnt offering and offered it by the rule.
17 Then he presented the grain offering. He took a handful and burned it on the altar. He did this in addition to the morning's burnt offering.
18 Then he killed the ox and the ram as the people's peace offering. His sons brought him the blood. He sprinkled it on all sides of the altar.
19 They also brought the fat parts from the ox and the ram — the fat tail, the fat covering the inside parts, the kidneys, and part of the liver.
20 They placed these on the breasts. Aaron burned the fat parts on the altar.
21 But he waved the breasts and the right thigh as a wave offering before the LORD. He did this as Moses had commanded.
22 Aaron lifted up his hands toward the people and blessed them. He had made the sin offering, the burnt offering, and the peace offering. Then he stepped down.
23 Moses and Aaron then entered the tent of meeting. When they came out, they blessed the people. And the glory of the LORD appeared to all the people.

24 Fire came out from the presence of the LORD. It burned up the burnt offering and the fat parts on the altar. When all the people saw it, they shouted for joy and fell with their faces to the ground.

title: Leviticus 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Aaron's sons Nadab and Abihu took their censers. They put fire in them and added incense. They offered unauthorized fire before the LORD, which he had not commanded.
2 So fire came out from the presence of the LORD and consumed them. They died before the LORD.

3 Then Moses said to Aaron, "This is what the LORD spoke of when he said: 'Among those who approach me I will be proved holy; in the sight of all the people I will be honored.'" Aaron remained silent.
4 Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan, sons of Aaron's uncle Uzziel. He said, "Come here. Carry your cousins outside the camp, away from the front of the holy place."
5 So they came and carried them, still in their tunics, outside the camp. They did as Moses had said.

6 Then Moses said to Aaron and his sons Eleazar and Ithamar, "Do not let your hair become unkempt and do not tear your clothes, or you will die and the LORD will be angry with the whole community. But your brothers, all the people of Israel, may mourn for those the LORD has burned.
7 Do not go outside the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, or you will die. The LORD's anointing oil is on you." So they did as Moses said.
8 Then the LORD said to Aaron,
9 "You and your sons must not drink wine or fermented drink when you enter the Tent of Meeting, or you will die. This is a lasting rule for all generations.
10 You must distinguish between the holy and the common, between the unclean and the clean.
11 And you must teach the people of Israel all the rules the LORD has given them through Moses."

12 Moses said to Aaron and his remaining sons, Eleazar and Ithamar, "Take the grain offering left from the offerings made by fire to the LORD. Eat it without yeast beside the altar, because it is most holy.
13 You shall eat it in a holy place. It is your share and your sons' share of the offerings made by fire to the LORD. This is what I have been commanded.

14 You and your sons and daughters may eat the breast of the wave offering and the right thigh given as a gift. Eat them in a clean place. These portions are for you and your children from the peace offerings of the people of Israel.

15 They must bring the thigh and the breast, with the fat portions of the offerings made by fire. They will wave them as a wave offering before the LORD. These belong to you and your children forever, as the LORD has commanded."
16 Later, Moses looked carefully for the goat of the sin offering, but it had been burned up. He was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron's remaining sons. He asked,
17 "Why didn't you eat the sin offering in the holy place? It is most holy. The LORD gave it to you to take away the guilt of the community and make things right with God for them before the LORD.
18 Since its blood was not brought inside the holy place, you should have eaten it in the sanctuary area, as I commanded."

19 But Aaron replied to Moses, "Look, today they brought their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD. But these things have happened to me. If I had eaten the sin offering today, would the LORD have been pleased?"
20 When Moses heard this, he was satisfied.

title: Leviticus 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron. He said,
2 "Say to the Israelites, 'Of all the land animals, these are the ones you may eat.
3 You may eat any animal that has a split hoof and chews the cud.
4 But you must not eat these animals. Some only chew the cud. Some only have a split hoof. The camel chews the cud, but it does not have a split hoof. It is unclean for you.
5 The rock badger chews the cud, but it does not have a split hoof. It is unclean for you.
6 The rabbit chews the cud, but it does not have a split hoof. It is unclean for you.
7 The pig has a split hoof, but it does not chew the cud. It is unclean for you.
8 You must not eat their meat. You must not touch their dead bodies. They are unclean for you.
9 Of all the creatures in the water, you may eat anything with fins and scales. This is true in the seas and in the streams.
10 But all the swarming things in the water are hateful to you. Anything in the seas or streams without fins and scales is hateful.
11 You must hate them. Do not eat their meat. Hate their dead bodies.
12 Everything in the water without fins and scales is hateful to you.
13 You must hate these birds. Do not eat them, because they are hateful: the eagle, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,
14 the kite, any kind of falcon,
15 any kind of raven,
16 the ostrich, the screech owl, the gull, any kind of hawk,
17 the little owl, the cormorant, the great owl,
18 the white owl, the desert owl, the osprey,
19 the stork, any kind of heron, the hoopoe, and the bat.
20 All winged insects that walk on all fours are hateful to you.
21 But you may eat some winged insects that walk on all fours. These are the ones with jointed legs above their feet for hopping on the ground.
22 Of these you may eat any kind of locust, katydid, cricket, or grasshopper.
23 All other winged insects with four legs are hateful to you.
24 These creatures will make you unclean. Anyone who touches their dead bodies will be unclean until evening.
25 Anyone who picks up one of their dead bodies must wash his clothes. He will be unclean until evening.
26 Every animal with a hoof that is not split, or that does not chew the cud, is unclean for you. Anyone who touches them will be unclean.
27 All four-footed animals that walk on their paws are unclean for you. Anyone who touches their dead bodies will be unclean until evening.
28 Anyone who picks up a dead body must wash his clothes. He will be unclean until evening. They are unclean for you.
29 These swarming things on the ground are unclean for you: the weasel, the rat, any kind of large lizard,
30 the gecko, the monitor lizard, the wall lizard, the skink, and the chameleon.
31 These animals are unclean for you among all swarming things. Anyone who touches them when they are dead will be unclean until evening.

32 When one of them dies and falls on something, that thing becomes unclean. This is true for any item made of wood, cloth, leather, or sackcloth. It is true for any work tool. You must rinse it with water. It will be unclean until evening. Then it will be clean.
33 If one of them falls into a clay pot, everything inside becomes unclean. You must break the pot.
34 Any food in that pot that has water on it will be unclean. Any drink in such a pot will be unclean.
35 Anything one of their dead bodies falls on will be unclean. If it is an oven or cooking pot, you must break it. It is unclean and will stay unclean for you.
36 But a spring or a cistern that holds water will stay clean. Yet anyone who touches a dead body in it will be unclean.
37 If a dead body falls on seed for planting, the seed is still clean.
38 But if water has been put on the seed, and a dead body falls on it, the seed is unclean for you.
39 If an animal that you may eat dies on its own, anyone who touches the body will be unclean until evening.
40 Anyone who eats from the body must wash his clothes. He will be unclean until evening. Anyone who picks up the body must wash his clothes. He will be unclean until evening.
41 Every creature that moves along the ground is hateful. You must not eat it.
42 Do not eat any creature that moves along the ground. This includes anything that crawls on its belly. It includes anything that walks on four or more feet. Such creatures are hateful.
43 Do not make yourselves unclean by any crawling creature. Do not become unclean or defiled by them.
44 For I am the LORD your God; consecrate yourselves and be holy, because I am holy. You must not make yourselves unclean by any creature that crawls along the ground.
45 For I am the LORD. I brought you up out of the land of Egypt to be your God. Therefore be holy, because I am holy.
46 This is the law about animals, birds, every living thing that moves in the water, and every creature that crawls along the ground.
47 You must distinguish between the unclean and the clean, between living creatures that may be eaten and those that may not be eaten.'"

title: Leviticus 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses.
2 "Say to the Israelites: 'A woman who has a baby boy will be unclean for seven days. She will be unclean like during her monthly period.
3 On the eighth day, the boy must be circumcised.
4 Then the woman must wait thirty-three more days for her purification from bleeding. She must not touch anything holy. She must not go into the sanctuary until her days of purification are complete.
5 But if she has a baby girl, the woman will be unclean for two weeks. She will be unclean like during her monthly period. Then she must wait sixty-six more days for her purification from bleeding.

6 When the days of her purification are complete, whether for a son or a daughter, she must bring offerings to the priest. She must come to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. She must bring a year-old lamb for a burnt offering. She must also bring a young pigeon or a dove for a sin offering.
7 The priest will present them before the LORD and make atonement for her. Then she will be made clean from her bleeding. This is the law for a woman who gives birth, whether to a boy or a girl.

8 But if she cannot afford a lamb, she must bring two doves or two young pigeons. One is for a burnt offering, and the other is for a sin offering. In this way the priest will make atonement for her, and she will be clean.'"

title: Leviticus 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
2 "When someone has a swelling, rash, or bright spot on the skin, it may be a defiling skin disease. He must be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons.
3 The priest will examine the spot on the skin. If the hair in it has turned white and the spot looks deeper than the skin, it is a defiling skin disease. The priest must declare him unclean.
4 But if the spot is white and does not look deeper than the skin, and the hair has not turned white, the priest will keep the person apart for seven days.
5 On the seventh day the priest will examine him again. If the spot looks the same and has not spread, the priest will keep him apart for another seven days.

6 On the seventh day the priest will examine him again. If the spot has faded and has not spread, the priest will declare him clean. It is only a rash. The person must wash his clothes, and he will be clean.
7 But if the rash spreads on the skin after the priest has declared him clean, he must show himself to the priest again.
8 The priest will examine him again. If the rash has spread, the priest must declare him unclean. He has a defiling skin disease.
9 When anyone has a defiling skin disease, he must be brought to the priest.
10 The priest will examine him. If there is a white swelling on the skin and the hair has turned white, and there is raw flesh in the swelling,
11 it is a long-lasting defiling skin disease. The priest must declare him unclean. He does not need to keep him apart, because he is already unclean.
12 But if the disease breaks out all over the skin and covers him from head to foot, as far as the priest can see,
13 the priest will examine him. If the disease has covered his whole body, the priest must declare him clean. Since it has all turned white, he is clean.
14 But when raw flesh appears on him, he will be unclean.
15 When the priest sees the raw flesh, he must declare him unclean. The raw flesh is unclean. It is a defiling skin disease.
16 But if the raw flesh changes and turns white, he must go to the priest.
17 The priest will examine him again. If the spot has turned white, the priest must declare him clean. Then he is clean.
18 When a boil appears on someone's skin and heals,
19 and a white swelling or a reddish-white spot grows where the boil was, he must show himself to the priest.
20 The priest will examine it. If it looks deeper than the skin and the hair has turned white, the priest must declare him unclean. It is a defiling skin disease that broke out in the boil.
21 But if the priest examines it and there is no white hair, and it is not deeper than the skin and has faded, the priest will keep him apart for seven days.
22 If it spreads on the skin, the priest must declare him unclean. It is an infection.
23 But if the spot stays the same and does not spread, it is only the scar from the boil. The priest must declare him clean.
24 When there is a burn on someone's skin, and the raw area of the burn turns reddish-white or white,

25 the priest must examine it. If the hair in the spot has turned white and the spot looks deeper than the skin, it is a defiling skin disease that broke out in the burn. The priest must declare him unclean.
26 But if the priest examines it and there is no white hair in the spot, and it is not deeper than the skin but has faded, the priest will keep him apart for seven days.
27 On the seventh day the priest will examine him again. If it has spread on the skin, the priest must declare him unclean. It is a defiling skin disease.
28 But if the spot stays the same and has not spread, but has faded, it is only a swelling from the burn. The priest must declare him clean. It is only the scar from the burn.
29 If a man or woman has an infection on the scalp or beard,

30 the priest will examine it. If it looks deeper than the skin and the hair in it is yellow and thin, the priest must declare the person unclean. It is a scaly infection, a defiling skin disease of the scalp or beard.
31 But if the priest examines the scaly infection and it does not look deeper than the skin, and there is no black hair in it, the priest will keep the person apart for seven days.
32 On the seventh day the priest will examine the infection again. If the scaly infection has not spread, and there is no yellow hair in it, and it does not look deeper than the skin,
33 the person must shave himself, but not the scaly area. Then the priest will keep him apart for another seven days.

34 On the seventh day the priest will examine the scaly infection again. If it has not spread on the skin and does not look deeper than the skin, the priest must declare him clean. He must wash his clothes, and he will be clean.
35 But if the scaly infection spreads on the skin after he has been declared clean,
36 the priest will examine him. If the scaly infection has spread, the priest does not need to look for yellow hair. The person is unclean.
37 But if in his sight the scaly infection has not changed, and black hair has grown in it, then it has healed. He is clean, and the priest must declare him clean.
38 When a man or woman has white spots on the skin,
39 the priest will examine them. If the spots are dull white, it is only a harmless rash that has broken out on the skin. The person is clean.
40 If a man loses the hair on his head and is bald, he is still clean.
41 If his hairline moves back and he is bald on the forehead, he is still clean.
42 But if there is a reddish-white sore on the bald head or forehead, it is a defiling skin disease breaking out on him.
43 The priest will examine him. If the swelling on his bald head or forehead is reddish-white like a defiling skin disease,
44 the man is diseased. He is unclean. The priest must declare him unclean because of the infection on his head.
45 The defiled person must wear torn clothes, let his hair be unkempt, cover the lower part of his face, and cry out, 'Unclean! Unclean!'
46 As long as he has the disease, he remains unclean. He must live alone. He must live outside the camp.
47 If any fabric has mildew on it — any wool or linen garment,
48 any woven or knit cloth of linen or wool, or any leather article —
49 and if the mark in the fabric, leather, woven cloth, or knit cloth is green or red, then it has mildew. It must be shown to the priest.
50 The priest will examine the mildew and keep the item apart for seven days.
51 On the seventh day the priest will examine it again. If the mildew has spread, it is a harmful mildew. The item is unclean.
52 He must burn the fabric, woven cloth, or knit cloth, whether it is wool, linen, or leather. The mildew is harmful, so the item must be burned up.
53 But if the priest examines it and the mildew has not spread on the fabric or leather,
54 the priest will order the item to be washed. Then it will be kept apart for another seven days.
55 After washing, the priest will examine it again. If the mildew has not changed in look, the item is unclean. Even if the mildew has not spread, you must burn it up.
56 If the priest examines it and the mildew has faded after washing, he must cut the spot out of the fabric or leather.
57 But if it appears again in the fabric or leather, it is spreading. You must burn the item up.
58 If the mildew is gone from the fabric or leather after washing, it must be washed again. Then it will be clean.
59 This is the law about mildew in wool or linen fabric, woven cloth, knit cloth, or leather, for declaring it clean or unclean."

title: Leviticus 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "This is the law for a person sick with a skin disease. It is for the day he is made clean and brought to the priest.
3 The priest must go outside the camp to examine him. If the skin disease has healed,
4 the priest will order two live clean birds. He will also order cedar wood, scarlet yarn, and hyssop.
5 Then the priest will command that one bird be killed. It must be killed over fresh water in a clay pot.
6 He will take the live bird with the cedar wood, scarlet yarn, and hyssop. He will dip them into the blood of the dead bird.
7 He will sprinkle the person seven times. Then he will say the person is clean. He will release the live bird into the open field.

8 The person being made clean must wash his clothes. He must shave off all his hair. He must wash his body with water. Then he will be clean. After that, he may enter the camp. But he must stay outside his tent for seven days.

9 On the seventh day, he must shave off all his hair again. This means his head, his beard, his eyebrows, and the rest of his hair. He must wash his clothes and wash his body with water. Then he will be clean.

10 On the eighth day, he must bring two male lambs without blemish. He must also bring one ewe lamb one year old without blemish. He must bring three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with olive oil as a grain offering. He must also bring one log of olive oil.
11 The priest who makes him clean will present him before the LORD. He will present him with these offerings at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
12 Then the priest will take one of the male lambs. He will present it as a guilt offering with the log of olive oil. He will wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.
13 He will kill the lamb in the holy place. This is where the sin offering and burnt offering are killed. Like the sin offering, the guilt offering belongs to the priest. It is most holy.
14 The priest will take some of the blood from the guilt offering. He will put it on the right ear-lobe of the one to be made clean. He will put it on the right thumb and the right big toe.
15 Then the priest will take some of the log of olive oil. He will pour it into his left palm.
16 He will dip his right finger into the oil in his left palm. He will sprinkle some of the oil seven times before the LORD.

17 The priest will put some of the oil left in his palm on the right ear-lobe of the one to be made clean. He will put it on the right thumb and the right big toe. He will put it on top of the blood of the guilt offering.
18 The rest of the oil in his palm, the priest will put on the head of the one to be made clean. This will make atonement for him before the LORD.
19 Then the priest will offer the sin offering. He will make atonement for the one being made clean from his uncleanness. After that, he will kill the burnt offering
20 and offer it on the altar with the grain offering. This will make atonement for him, and he will be clean.

21 If the person is poor and cannot afford these offerings, he must bring less. He will take one male lamb as a guilt offering to be waved for him. He must also bring a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with olive oil as a grain offering. He must also bring a log of olive oil.
22 He must bring two doves or two young pigeons, whichever he can afford. One will be a sin offering. The other will be a burnt offering.
23 On the eighth day, he must bring them for his cleansing. He will bring them to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting before the LORD.
24 The priest will take the lamb for the guilt offering with the log of olive oil. He will wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.

25 Then he will kill the lamb for the guilt offering. The priest will take some of the blood. He will put it on the right ear-lobe of the one to be made clean. He will put it on the right thumb and the right big toe.
26 Then the priest will pour some of the oil into his left palm.
27 He will sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil in his left palm. He will do this seven times before the LORD.

28 The priest will also put some of the oil in his palm on the right ear-lobe of the one to be made clean. He will put it on the right thumb and the right big toe. He will put it on the same places as the blood of the guilt offering.
29 The rest of the oil in his palm, the priest will put on the head of the one to be made clean. This will make atonement for him before the LORD.
30 Then he must offer the doves or young pigeons, whichever he can afford.
31 One will be a sin offering. The other will be a burnt offering with the grain offering. In this way, the priest will make atonement before the LORD for the one to be made clean.
32 This is the law for a person with a skin disease who cannot afford the cost of his cleansing."
33 Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
34 "You will soon enter the land of Canaan. I am giving it to you as your possession. I may put mildew into a house in that land.
35 Then the owner of the house must come and tell the priest. He must say, 'Something like mildew has appeared in my house.'
36 The priest must order that the house be cleared before he enters it. This way, nothing in the house will become unclean. After that, the priest will go in to inspect the house.
37 He will examine the house. He will look for mildew on the walls. If he sees green or red marks that go deep into the wall,
38 the priest must go outside the doorway of the house. He must close it up for seven days.
39 On the seventh day, the priest will return and inspect the house. If the mildew has spread on the walls,
40 he must order the contaminated stones to be pulled out. They must be thrown into an unclean place outside the city.
41 He must have the inside of the house scraped completely. The plaster that is scraped off must be dumped outside the city in an unclean place.
42 Then new stones must be brought to replace the old ones. New plaster must also be brought to cover the house.
43 Sometimes the mildew comes back after the stones are removed. It may come back after the house is scraped and replastered.
44 Then the priest must come and inspect it. If the mildew has spread in the house, it is mildew that ruins the house. The house is unclean.
45 The house must be torn down. Its stones, its wooden beams, and all its plaster must be taken outside the city to an unclean place.
46 Anyone who enters the house while it is closed up will be unclean until evening.
47 Anyone who sleeps in the house or eats in it must wash his clothes.
48 But if the priest comes and inspects the house, the mildew may not have spread after the house was replastered. Then he will say the house is clean. The mildew is gone.
49 To purify the house, he will take two birds, cedar wood, scarlet yarn, and hyssop.
50 He will kill one of the birds over fresh water in a clay pot.
51 Then he will take the cedar wood, the hyssop, the scarlet yarn, and the live bird. He will dip them in the blood of the dead bird and the fresh water. He will sprinkle the house seven times.
52 In this way, he will cleanse the house. He will use the bird's blood, the fresh water, the live bird, the cedar wood, the hyssop, and the scarlet yarn.
53 Finally, he will release the live bird into the open field outside the city. In this way, he will make atonement for the house, and it will be clean.
54 This is the law for any skin disease that makes unclean, for a scaly rash,
55 for mildew in clothing or in a house,
56 and for a swelling, rash, or shiny spot,
57 to decide when something is clean or unclean. This is the law about skin diseases and mildew."

title: Leviticus 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
2 "Tell the Israelites this: When a man has an abnormal discharge from his body, that discharge is unclean.
3 He is unclean from his discharge. It does not matter if it flows or is blocked. The discharge makes him unclean.
4 Any bed the man lies on will be unclean. Anything he sits on will be unclean.
5 Anyone who touches his bed must wash his clothes. He must bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
6 Anyone who sits on something the man sat on must wash his clothes. He must bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
7 Anyone who touches the man's body must wash his clothes. He must bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
8 If the man spits on someone who is clean, that person must wash his clothes. He must bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
9 Any saddle the man rides on will be unclean.
10 Anyone who touches something that was under him will be unclean until evening. Anyone who carries such things must wash his clothes. He must bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
11 The man must rinse his hands with water before he touches anyone. If he does not, the one he touched must wash his clothes. That person must bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
12 Any clay pot the man touches must be broken. Any wooden tool must be rinsed with water.
13 When the man's discharge stops, he must count seven days. Then he washes his clothes and bathes in fresh water. After that, he is clean.
14 On the eighth day, he must take two doves or two young pigeons. He must come before the LORD at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. He must give the birds to the priest.
15 The priest will offer one bird as a sin offering. He will offer the other as a burnt offering. In this way, the priest makes things right with God for the man because of his discharge.
16 When a man has a release of semen, he must bathe his whole body with water. He will be unclean until evening.
17 Any clothing or leather with semen on it must be washed with water. It will be unclean until evening.
18 When a man and woman have sex and there is a release of semen, both must bathe with water. They will be unclean until evening.
19 When a woman has her monthly period, she will be unclean for seven days. Anyone who touches her will be unclean until evening.
20 Anything she lies on during her period will be unclean. Anything she sits on will be unclean.
21 Anyone who touches her bed must wash his clothes. He must bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
22 Anyone who touches anything she sat on must wash his clothes. He must bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
23 This is true for her bed or anything she sat on. Whoever touches it will be unclean until evening.
24 If a man has sex with her and her blood touches him, he will be unclean for seven days. Any bed he lies on will become unclean.
25 Sometimes a woman bleeds for many days when it is not her period. Or her period lasts longer than normal. She will be unclean during all those days, just like during her period.
26 Any bed she lies on during this time will be unclean. Anything she sits on will be unclean, just like her bed during her period.
27 Anyone who touches these things will be unclean. He must wash his clothes and bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening.
28 When the woman's bleeding stops, she must count seven days. After that, she will be clean before God.
29 On the eighth day, she must take two doves or two young pigeons. She must bring them to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
30 The priest will offer one bird as a sin offering. He will offer the other as a burnt offering. In this way, the priest makes things right with God for her because of her unclean discharge.
31 You must keep the Israelites separate from things that make them unclean. If you do not, they will die in their uncleanness for making my dwelling place unclean. My dwelling place is among them.
32 This is the law for a man who has a discharge. It is also for a man who has a release of semen that makes him unclean.
33 It is also for a woman in her monthly period. It is for any man or woman who has a discharge. And it is for a man who has sex with an unclean woman."

title: Leviticus 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 The LORD spoke to Moses after Aaron's two sons died. They had come too close to the LORD's presence.

2 The LORD said to Moses: "Tell your brother Aaron not to come freely into the Most Holy Place. It is behind the curtain, in front of the mercy seat on the ark. If he comes whenever he wants, he will die. For I appear in the cloud above the mercy seat.
3 This is how Aaron must enter the Holy Place. He must bring a young bull for a sin offering. He must also bring a ram for a burnt offering.

4 He must wear the holy linen shirt and linen underclothes. He must tie a linen belt around him. He must put on the linen head cloth. These are holy clothes. He must bathe with water before he wears them.
5 He shall take two male goats from the community of Israel for a sin offering. He shall also take one ram for a burnt offering.
6 Aaron will offer the bull for his sin offering. This will make atonement for himself and his household.
7 Then he shall take the two goats. He shall present them before the LORD at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
8 Aaron will cast lots for the two goats. One lot is for the LORD. The other lot is for the scapegoat.
9 Aaron shall bring the goat chosen by lot for the LORD. He will offer it as a sin offering.
10 But the goat chosen as the scapegoat shall be kept alive before the LORD. It will be used to make atonement. Then it will be sent into the desert as the scapegoat.
11 Aaron will present the bull for his own sin offering. He will make atonement for himself and his household. Then he must kill the bull for his sin offering.
12 Then he must take a fire pan full of burning coals from the altar before the LORD. He must also take two handfuls of fine, sweet-smelling incense. He must carry them behind the curtain.
13 He shall put the incense on the fire before the LORD. The cloud of incense will cover the mercy seat above the tablets of God's law. Then he will not die.
14 He must take some of the bull's blood. He shall sprinkle it with his finger on the east side of the mercy seat. He shall also sprinkle some of it seven times before the mercy seat.

15 Then Aaron shall kill the goat for the people's sin offering. He must bring its blood behind the curtain. He must do with this blood as he did with the bull's blood. He shall sprinkle it on the mercy seat and in front of it.

16 So he shall make atonement for the Most Holy Place. The Israelites have made it unclean by their sins and rebellion. He must do the same for the Tent of Meeting. It stands among them, in the middle of their unclean ways.

17 No one may be in the Tent of Meeting during this time. This is from when Aaron goes in to make atonement until he comes out. He must first make atonement for himself, his household, and all Israel.
18 Then he shall go out to the altar before the LORD. He will make atonement for it. He must take some of the bull's blood and some of the goat's blood. He shall put it on all the horns of the altar.
19 He shall sprinkle some of the blood on it with his finger seven times. This will cleanse it. It will set it apart from the unclean ways of the Israelites.
20 When Aaron has finished cleansing the Most Holy Place, the Tent of Meeting, and the altar, he must bring forward the live goat.

21 Then he shall lay both his hands on the head of the live goat. He shall confess over it all the wrong deeds and rebellion of the Israelites — all their sins. He shall put them on the goat's head. Then he shall send it into the desert. A man chosen for this task will lead it away.
22 The goat will carry on itself all their wrong deeds into an empty place. The man will release it into the desert.
23 Then Aaron shall enter the Tent of Meeting. He shall take off the linen clothes he wore in the Most Holy Place. He must leave them there.

24 He shall bathe with water in a holy place. Then he shall put on his own clothes. He must go out and offer his burnt offering and the people's burnt offering. This will make atonement for himself and for the people.
25 He shall also burn the fat of the sin offering on the altar.
26 The man who released the goat as the scapegoat must wash his clothes. He must bathe with water. After that, he may come back into the camp.

27 The bull for the sin offering and the goat for the sin offering must be taken outside the camp. Their blood was brought into the Most Holy Place for atonement. Their skins, meat, and waste must be burned up.
28 The man who burns them must wash his clothes and bathe with water. After that, he may come back into the camp.

29 This is to be a lasting rule for you. On the tenth day of the seventh month, you shall deny yourselves. You must not do any work. This is for the native-born and for the foreigner living among you.
30 On this day, atonement will be made for you to cleanse you. Then you will be clean from all your sins before the LORD.
31 It is a Sabbath of complete rest for you. You shall deny yourselves. It is a lasting rule.
32 The priest who is chosen and set apart with oil to follow his father as high priest will make atonement. He will put on the holy linen clothes.
33 He will make atonement for the Most Holy Place, the Tent of Meeting, and the altar. He will also make atonement for the priests and all the people of the community.
34 This is to be a lasting rule for you. Atonement must be made once a year for the Israelites because of all their sins." And all this was done, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

title: Leviticus 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to Aaron, his sons, and all the Israelites. Tell them this is what the LORD has commanded:
3 'Anyone from Israel who kills an ox, a lamb, or a goat in the camp or outside it,
4 but does not bring it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting as an offering to the LORD — that man is guilty of shedding blood. He must be cut off from his people.

5 So the Israelites must bring their sacrifices to the LORD. They must not offer them in the open fields anymore. They must bring them to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. There they will offer them as peace offerings to the LORD.
6 The priest will then sprinkle the blood on the altar of the LORD at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He will burn the fat as a pleasing smell to the LORD.
7 They must no longer offer their sacrifices to the goat idols (demons). They have given themselves to these idols. This will be a lasting law for them for all generations.'
8 "Tell them this too. If anyone from Israel or any foreigner who lives with them offers a burnt offering or a sacrifice,
9 but does not bring it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting to sacrifice it to the LORD — that man must be cut off from his people.
10 If anyone from Israel or a foreigner who lives with them eats any blood, I will set my face against that person. I will cut him off from his people.
11 For the life of a creature is in the blood. I have given it to you to make atonement for yourselves on the altar. It is the blood that makes atonement for one's life.
12 Therefore I say to the Israelites, 'None of you may eat blood. No foreigner who lives with you may eat blood either.'
13 "If any Israelite or foreigner who lives with them hunts a wild animal or bird that may be eaten, he must pour out its blood and cover it with dirt.

14 For the life of every creature is its blood. So I have told the Israelites, 'You must not eat the blood of any living thing. The life of every creature is its blood. Whoever eats it must be cut off.'

15 "Any person, whether a native Israelite or a foreigner, may find something already dead or torn by wild animals. If he eats it, he must wash his clothes and bathe with water. He will be unclean until evening. Then he will be clean.
16 But if he does not wash his clothes and bathe himself, then he must carry his guilt."

title: Leviticus 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to the Israelites and tell them: I am the LORD your God.
3 You must not do as they do in Egypt, where you used to live. You must not do as they do in the land of Canaan, where I am bringing you. Do not follow their practices.
4 You must obey my laws and be careful to follow my decrees. I am the LORD your God.
5 Keep my decrees and laws, for the person who obeys them will live by them. I am the LORD.
6 No one is to have sexual relations with any close relative. I am the LORD.
7 Do not have sexual relations with your mother. She is your mother; do not have sexual relations with her. This would dishonor your father.
8 Do not have sexual relations with your father's wife; that would dishonor your father.
9 Do not have sexual relations with your sister, whether she is your father's daughter or your mother's daughter. It does not matter if she was born in the same home or elsewhere.
10 Do not have sexual relations with your son's daughter or your daughter's daughter. That would shame your family.
11 Do not have sexual relations with the daughter of your father's wife, born to your father. She is your sister.
12 Do not have sexual relations with your father's sister. She is your father's close relative.
13 Do not have sexual relations with your mother's sister. She is your mother's close relative.
14 Do not dishonor your father's brother by having sexual relations with his wife. She is your aunt.
15 Do not have sexual relations with your daughter-in-law. She is your son's wife; do not have sexual relations with her.
16 Do not have sexual relations with your brother's wife; that would shame your brother.

17 Do not have sexual relations with both a woman and her daughter. Do not marry her son's daughter or her daughter's daughter and have sexual relations with her. They are close relatives; it is wicked.
18 Do not take your wife's sister as a rival wife. Do not have sexual relations with her while your wife is still alive.
19 Do not have sexual relations with a woman during her monthly period.
20 Do not have sexual relations with your neighbor's wife and defile yourself with her.
21 Do not give any of your children to be sacrificed to Molek, for you must not profane the name of your God. I am the LORD.
22 Do not have sexual relations with a man as one does with a woman; that is detestable.
23 Do not have sexual relations with an animal and defile yourself with it. A woman must not offer herself to an animal for sexual relations; that is wicked.
24 Do not defile yourselves in any of these ways. This is how the nations that I am driving out before you became defiled.
25 Even the land was defiled; so I punished it for its sin, and the land vomited out its inhabitants.
26 But you must keep my decrees and laws. You must not do any of these detestable things — not the native-born and not the foreigner living among you.
27 The people who lived in the land before you did all these detestable things. So the land became defiled.
28 If you defile the land, it will vomit you out, just as it vomited out the nations before you.
29 Anyone who does any of these detestable things must be cut off from his people.
30 Keep my command. Do not follow any of the detestable customs that were practiced before you. Do not defile yourselves with them. I am the LORD your God."

title: Leviticus 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to all the people of Israel. Tell them: Be holy because I, the LORD your God, am holy.
3 Each of you must respect his mother and father. You must also keep my Sabbaths. I am the LORD your God.
4 Do not turn to idols. Do not make metal gods for yourselves. I am the LORD your God.
5 When you give a peace offering to the LORD, offer it so it will be accepted.
6 Eat it on the day you offer it, or on the next day. But you must burn up anything left on the third day.
7 If any of it is eaten on the third day, it is bad. The LORD will not accept it.
8 Anyone who eats it will carry the guilt. He has dishonored what is holy to the LORD. That person must be cut off from his people.
9 When you reap the harvest of your land, do not cut all the way to the edges of your field. Do not pick up the fallen grain.
10 Do not go over your vineyard a second time. Do not pick up the grapes that have fallen. Leave them for the poor and the foreigner. I am the LORD your God.
11 Do not steal. Do not lie or cheat each other.
12 Do not make false promises in my name. That brings shame to the name of your God. I am the LORD.
13 Do not cheat your neighbor. Do not rob him. Do not hold back a worker's pay until morning.
14 Do not curse a deaf person. Do not put something in front of a blind person to trip them. Fear your God. I am the LORD.
15 Do not be unfair in court. Do not take the side of the poor or the rich. Judge your neighbor fairly.
16 Do not spread lies about your people. Do not do anything that puts your neighbor's life in danger. I am the LORD.
17 Do not hate your brother in your heart. Tell your neighbor clearly when he is wrong. Then you will not share his guilt.
18 Do not seek revenge. Do not stay angry with any of your people. But love your neighbor as yourself. I am the LORD.
19 You must keep my rules. Do not breed two kinds of animals together. Do not plant your fields with two kinds of seed. Do not wear clothing made of two kinds of cloth.

20 Suppose a man has sex with a slave girl. She is promised to another man, but she has not been bought back or set free. There must be a punishment. But they must not be put to death, because she was not yet free.
21 The man must bring a ram to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. It is his guilt offering to the LORD.
22 The priest will pay for the sin with the ram of the guilt offering. Then the man will be forgiven for what he did.
23 When you enter the land, plant fruit trees. For three years you must not eat their fruit. The fruit is forbidden.
24 In the fourth year, all the fruit will be set apart to praise the LORD.
25 In the fifth year, you may eat the fruit. Then your harvest will grow more. I am the LORD your God.
26 Do not eat any meat with the blood still in it. Do not tell the future or do magic.
27 Do not cut off the hair at the sides of your head. Do not clip the edges of your beard.
28 Do not cut your bodies for the dead. Do not put tattoo marks on yourselves. I am the LORD.
29 Do not bring shame on your daughter by making her a prostitute. If you do, the land will be full of sex sin and wickedness.
30 You must keep my Sabbaths. You must show deep respect for my holy place. I am the LORD.
31 Do not turn to mediums or spiritists. Do not look for them. They will make you unclean. I am the LORD your God.
32 Stand up in the presence of old people. Show respect for the aged. Fear your God. I am the LORD.
33 When a foreigner lives among you in your land, do not treat him badly.
34 Treat the foreigner among you like one of your own people. Love him as yourself. Remember, you were foreigners in Egypt. I am the LORD your God.
35 Do not use wrong measures for length, weight, or volume.
36 Use honest scales and honest weights. Use an honest ephah and an honest hin. I am the LORD your God. I brought you out of Egypt.
37 Keep all my rules and all my laws. Follow them. I am the LORD."

title: Leviticus 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Tell the Israelites: 'Any Israelite or any foreigner living in Israel must die if he gives any of his children to Molek. The people of the land must kill him with stones.
3 I will set my face against that person. I will cut him off from his people. He gave his child to Molek. He made my holy place unclean. He made my holy name unclean.
4 What if the people of the land close their eyes? What if they do not put that man to death after he gives his child to Molek?
5 Then I myself will set my face against him and his family. I will cut him off from his people. I will also cut off everyone who follows him in being unfaithful with Molek.
6 I will also set my face against anyone who turns to mediums or spiritists. They are unfaithful by following them. I will cut that person off from his people.
7 So set yourselves apart and be holy. I am the LORD your God.
8 Keep my rules and obey them. I am the LORD who makes you holy.
9 Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death. He has cursed his father or mother. He is guilty of his own death.
10 If a man sleeps with another man's wife — with the wife of his neighbor — both the man and the woman must be put to death.
11 If a man sleeps with his father's wife, he has shamed his father. Both must be put to death. They are guilty of their own deaths.
12 If a man sleeps with his daughter-in-law, both must be put to death. They have done an evil act. They are guilty of their own deaths.
13 If a man sleeps with a man as with a woman, they have both done a hateful thing. Both must be put to death. They are guilty of their own deaths.
14 If a man marries both a woman and her mother, it is very evil. Both he and they must be burned in the fire. There must be no such evil among you.
15 If a man has sex with an animal, he must be put to death. You must also kill the animal.
16 If a woman goes near any animal to mate with it, kill both the woman and the animal. They must be put to death. They are guilty of their own deaths.

17 If a man marries his sister — the daughter of his father or his mother — and they have sexual relations, it is a shameful thing. They must be cut off in front of their people. He has shamed his sister. He must carry his guilt.
