18 If a man sleeps with a woman during her monthly period, he has uncovered her flow of blood. She has uncovered it too. Both must be cut off from their people.
19 You must not have sexual relations with the sister of your mother or your father. That brings shame on your own family. Both must carry their guilt.
20 If a man sleeps with his uncle's wife, he has shamed his uncle. They will carry their sin. They will die with no children.
21 If a man marries his brother's wife, it is an unclean act. He has shamed his brother. They will have no children.
22 Keep all my rules and laws and follow them. Then the land where I am bringing you to live will not throw you out.
23 Do not follow the practices of the nations I am going to drive out before you. They did all these things. So I hated them.
24 But I have told you that you will receive their land. I will give it to you as a gift to keep — a rich land full of milk and honey. I am the LORD your God. I have set you apart from the nations.

25 So you must make a difference between clean and unclean animals and birds. Do not make yourselves unclean by any animal or bird or anything that moves along the ground. I have set these apart as unclean for you.
26 You are to be holy to me, because I, the LORD, am holy. I have set you apart from the nations to be my own.
27 A man or a woman who is a medium or a spiritist must be put to death. They must be killed with stones. They are guilty of their own deaths.'"

title: Leviticus 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Speak to Aaron's sons, the priests. Tell them this: A priest must not make himself unclean by touching a dead person among his people.
2 But he may do so for a close family member — his mother, father, son, daughter, or brother.
3 He may also do so for his unmarried sister who is close to him, since she has no husband.
4 He must not make himself unclean for family by marriage. That would dishonor him.
5 Priests must not shave bald spots on their heads. They must not shave the edges of their beards. They must not cut their bodies.
6 They must be holy to their God. They must not dishonor the name of their God. They present the food offerings burned for the LORD. So they must be holy.
7 A priest must not marry a woman who has worked as a prostitute. He must not marry a divorced woman. The priest is holy to his God.
8 Treat him as holy. He presents the food of your God. He must be holy to you, because I the LORD am holy. I set you apart.
9 A priest's daughter may dishonor herself by becoming a prostitute. She dishonors her father. She must be burned in the fire.

10 One priest is the highest among his brothers. The holy oil has been poured on his head. He has been set apart to wear the priestly clothes. He must not let his hair hang loose. He must not tear his clothes.
11 He must not go near any dead body. He must not make himself unclean, even for his father or mother.
12 He must not leave the sanctuary of his God. He must not dishonor it. The holy oil of his God has set him apart. I am the LORD.
13 The woman he marries must be a virgin.
14 He must not marry a widow, a divorced woman, or a woman who has worked as a prostitute. He must marry a virgin from his own people.
15 Then he will not dishonor his children among his people. I am the LORD who makes him holy."
16 Then the LORD said to Moses,
17 "Say to Aaron: 'For all future generations, none of your descendants with a physical defect may come near to offer the food of his God.
18 No man with any defect may come near — no man who is blind or lame. No man with a damaged face or body.
19 No man with a broken foot or hand.
20 No man who is a hunchback or has a very short body. No man with an eye problem, infected sores, or scabs. No man with a damaged testicle.
21 No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a defect may come near. He must not present the food offerings burned for the LORD. He has a defect. He must not come near to offer the food of his God.
22 He may eat the most holy food of his God. He may also eat the holy food.
23 But he must not go near the curtain. He must not come near the altar. He has a defect. He must not dishonor my sanctuaries. I am the LORD who makes them holy.'"
24 So Moses told this to Aaron, to his sons, and to all the Israelites.

title: Leviticus 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Tell Aaron and his sons to treat the sacred offerings with respect. The Israelites have set these offerings apart for me. Aaron and his sons must not dishonor my holy name. I am the LORD.

3 Tell them this for all future generations. Any of their descendants in an unclean state must not come near the sacred offerings. The Israelites have set these offerings apart for the LORD. That person must be sent away from my presence. I am the LORD.

4 A descendant of Aaron may have a skin disease or a bodily discharge. He must not eat the sacred offerings until he is clean. The same rule applies if he touches anything made unclean by a dead body. It also applies if he touches a man with fluid from his body,
5 or if he touches a crawling creature that makes him unclean. It also applies if he touches a person who makes him unclean — for any reason.
6 The priest who touches any of these will be unclean until evening. He must not eat the sacred offerings unless he bathes with water.
7 When the sun has set, he will be clean. Then he may eat the sacred offerings, for they are his food.
8 He must not eat anything found dead. He must not eat anything torn by wild animals. These would make him unclean. I am the LORD.
9 The priests must keep my command. If they do not, they will carry guilt and die. They would dishonor the sacred offerings. I am the LORD who makes them holy.
10 No one outside a priest's family may eat the sacred offering. A guest of a priest may not eat it. A hired worker may not eat it either.
11 But a priest may buy a slave with his own money. Or a slave may be born in his household. That slave may eat the priest's food.
12 If a priest's daughter marries a man who is not a priest, she must not eat the sacred offerings.
13 But a priest's daughter may become a widow or be divorced. If she has no children and returns to her father's house, she may share her father's food as before. But no outsider may share it.
14 If anyone eats a sacred offering by mistake, he must pay for it. He must add one fifth to its value. Then he must give the payment to the priest.
15 The priests must not dishonor the sacred offerings that the Israelites give to the LORD.
16 They must not let people eat the sacred offerings. That would make the people carry the guilt. For I am the LORD who makes them holy."
17 Then the LORD said to Moses,

18 "Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the Israelites. Tell them this: 'Any man of Israel or any foreigner living among you may bring a gift. He may bring a burnt offering to the LORD. He may bring it to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering.
19 He must offer a male without defect. It must come from the cattle, sheep, or goats. Then it will be accepted on your behalf.
20 You must not present any animal with a defect. It will not be accepted on your behalf.
21 A man may present a peace offering to the LORD from the herd or flock. He may bring it to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering. It must be without defect to be acceptable.

22 You must not present to the LORD any animal that is blind. You must not present any animal that is injured in body or maimed. You must not present any animal with sores that run or sores that fester, or with scabs. You must not put any of these on the altar as an offering made by fire to the LORD.
23 You may present an ox or sheep with a deformed or short limb as a freewill offering. But you must not bring such an animal to fulfill a vow.
24 You must not offer to the LORD an animal whose testicles are bruised or crushed or torn or cut. You must not sacrifice such animals in your land.
25 You must not offer animals that foreign hands have handled as food to your God. They will not be accepted on your behalf. They are deformed and flawed.'"
26 Then the LORD said to Moses,
27 "When an ox, a sheep, or a goat is born, it must stay with its mother for seven days. From the eighth day on, it will be acceptable as an offering made by fire to the LORD.
28 But you must not slaughter a cow or a sheep on the same day as its young.
29 When you offer a thanksgiving sacrifice to the LORD, offer it so it will be accepted on your behalf.
30 It must be eaten that same day. Do not leave any of it until morning. I am the LORD.
31 Keep my commands and follow them. I am the LORD.
32 Do not dishonor my holy name. I must be acknowledged as holy by the Israelites. I am the LORD, who makes you holy,
33 who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the LORD."

title: Leviticus 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to the Israelites. Tell them, 'These are my set feasts, the feasts of the LORD. You are to announce them as sacred assemblies.
3 You may work for six days. But the seventh day is a Sabbath of complete rest. It is a day of sacred assembly. You must not do any work. Wherever you live, it is a Sabbath to the LORD.
4 These are the LORD's set feasts. You must announce them as sacred assemblies at their set times.
5 The Passover to the LORD begins at sunset on the fourteenth day of the first month.
6 On the fifteenth day of the same month, the Feast of Unleavened Bread to the LORD begins. For seven days you must eat bread made without yeast.
7 On the first day, you must hold a sacred assembly. You must not do any regular work.
8 For seven days you must present an offering made by fire to the LORD. On the seventh day there will be a sacred assembly. You must not do any regular work.'"
9 And the LORD said to Moses,
10 "Speak to the Israelites and say, 'You will enter the land that I am giving you. When you reap its harvest, bring the priest a sheaf of grain. It must be from the firstfruits of your harvest.
11 The priest will wave the sheaf before the LORD. Then it will be accepted on your behalf. He will wave it on the day after the Sabbath.
12 On the day you wave the sheaf, you must offer a male lamb a year old, without defect. It will be a burnt offering to the LORD.

13 Bring with it a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil. It is an offering made by fire to the LORD, a pleasing smell. Also bring a drink offering of a quarter hin of wine.
14 Do not eat any bread, or roasted or new grain, until the very day you bring this offering to your God. This is to be a lasting rule for all generations, wherever you live.
15 From the day after the Sabbath, count off seven full weeks. That is the day you brought the sheaf for the wave offering.
16 Count off fifty days, until the day after the seventh Sabbath. Then present a new grain offering to the LORD.
17 From your homes, bring two loaves of bread as a wave offering. Each loaf must be made from two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour. They must be baked with yeast. They are firstfruits to the LORD.

18 Along with the bread, present seven male lambs a year old, without defect. Also bring one young bull and two rams. They will be a burnt offering to the LORD. Bring them with their grain offerings and drink offerings. It is an offering made by fire, a pleasing smell to the LORD.
19 You must also prepare one male goat as a sin offering. And bring two male lambs a year old as a peace offering.
20 The priest will wave the lambs as a wave offering before the LORD. He will wave them with the bread of the firstfruits. The bread and the two lambs will be holy to the LORD for the priest.
21 On that same day, you must announce a sacred assembly. You must not do any regular work. This is to be a lasting rule wherever you live, for all generations.

22 When you reap the harvest of your land, do not reap to the very edges of your field. Do not gather the grain you missed. Leave it for the poor and the foreigner living among you. I am the LORD your God.'"
23 The LORD also said to Moses,
24 "Speak to the Israelites and say, 'On the first day of the seventh month, you must have a day of rest. It will be a sacred assembly announced by trumpet blasts.
25 You must not do any regular work. But you must present an offering made by fire to the LORD.'"
26 Again the LORD said to Moses,
27 "The tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. You must hold a sacred assembly and deny yourselves. Present an offering made by fire to the LORD.
28 On this day you must not do any work. It is the Day of Atonement. On this day, atonement is made for you before the LORD your God.
29 Those who do not deny themselves on that day must be cut off from their people.
30 I will destroy from among their people anyone who does any work on that day.
31 You must not do any work at all. This is a lasting rule for all generations, wherever you live.
32 It will be a Sabbath of complete rest for you. You must deny yourselves. From the evening of the ninth day of the month until the next evening, you must keep your Sabbath."
33 And the LORD said to Moses,
34 "Speak to the Israelites and say, 'On the fifteenth day of the seventh month, the Feast of Tabernacles to the LORD begins. It continues for seven days.
35 On the first day there will be a sacred assembly. You must not do any regular work.

36 For seven days you must present an offering made by fire to the LORD. On the eighth day, hold a sacred assembly. Present an offering made by fire to the LORD. It is a solemn assembly. You must not do any regular work.

37 These are the LORD's set feasts. You must announce them as sacred assemblies for offerings by fire to the LORD. They include burnt offerings, grain offerings, sacrifices, and drink offerings. Each one is for its set day.
38 These offerings are in addition to the offerings for the LORD's Sabbaths. They are also in addition to your gifts, your vow offerings, and your freewill offerings.

39 On the fifteenth day of the seventh month, after you gather the produce of the land, celebrate a feast to the LORD for seven days. There must be complete rest on the first day and also on the eighth day.
40 On the first day, take the fruit of fine trees, the branches of palm trees, the boughs of leafy trees, and willows from the stream. Rejoice before the LORD your God for seven days.
41 Celebrate this as a feast to the LORD for seven days each year. This is a lasting rule for all generations. Celebrate it in the seventh month.
42 Live in temporary shelters for seven days. All those born in Israel must live in temporary shelters,
43 so your descendants will know that I had the Israelites live in temporary shelters when I brought them out of the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.'"
44 So Moses announced the set feasts of the LORD to the Israelites.

title: Leviticus 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Command the Israelites to bring you clear oil of pressed olives for the light. Keep the lamps burning all the time.

3 Aaron is to tend the lamps outside the veil of the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting. He must keep them burning before the LORD from evening to morning continually. This is a lasting rule for all generations to come.
4 He shall tend the lamps on the pure gold lampstand before the LORD continually.
5 "Take fine flour and bake twelve loaves of bread of the Presence. Use two-tenths of an ephah for each loaf.
6 Set them in two rows — six per row — on the table of pure gold before the LORD.
7 Place pure frankincense beside each row. It will serve as a reminder portion for the bread. It is an offering made by fire to the LORD.
8 Every Sabbath day, set this bread before the LORD. This is for the Israelites, as a lasting covenant.

9 This is a lasting covenant for the Israelites. It belongs to Aaron and his sons. They must eat it in a holy place. It is a most holy part of the offerings made by fire to the LORD. It is his portion forever."
10 Now there was a young man among the Israelites. His mother was an Israelite, but his father was Egyptian. He went out into the camp. A fight broke out between him and an Israelite.
11 The son of the Israelite woman blasphemed the Name with a curse. So they brought him to Moses. (His mother's name was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan.)
12 They held him in custody until the LORD's will should be made clear to them.
13 Then the LORD said to Moses,
14 "Take the blasphemer outside the camp. All those who heard him are to lay their hands on his head. Then the entire assembly is to stone him.
15 Say to the Israelites: 'Anyone who curses their God will be held responsible.
16 Anyone who blasphemes the name of the LORD is to be put to death. The whole assembly must stone him. This is true for foreigner and native-born alike. If he blasphemes the Name, he must die.
17 Anyone who takes the life of a human being is to be put to death.
18 Anyone who takes the life of someone's animal must make restitution — life for life.
19 Anyone who injures their neighbor is to be injured in the same manner:
20 broken bone for broken bone, eye for eye, tooth for tooth. The one who has inflicted the injury must suffer the same injury.
21 Whoever kills an animal must pay back for it. But whoever kills a person must be put to death.
22 You must have the same law for the foreigner and the native-born. For I am the LORD your God.'"
23 Then Moses spoke to the Israelites. They took the blasphemer outside the camp and stoned him. So the Israelites did just as the LORD had commanded Moses.

title: Leviticus 25
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai. He said,
2 "Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you enter the land I am giving you, the land itself must keep a Sabbath to the LORD.
3 For six years you may plant your field. You may cut back your vineyard and gather its crops.
4 But in the seventh year the land must have full rest. It is a Sabbath to the LORD. Do not plant your field. Do not cut back your vineyard.
5 Do not harvest what grows by itself. Do not gather grapes from the vines you did not care for. The land must have a year of full rest.
6 Whatever the land grows during the Sabbath year is food for you. It is also for your male and female servants, your hired worker, and any foreigner who stays with you,
7 for your farm animals, and for the wild animals in your land. All that grows may be food.
8 Count off seven Sabbaths of years — seven times seven years. This will be forty-nine years in all.
9 Then sound the trumpet far and wide. Do this on the tenth day of the seventh month, the Day of Atonement. Sound the trumpet throughout your land.

10 Set the fiftieth year apart as holy. Proclaim liberty throughout the land to all who live in it. This will be a Jubilee for you. Each of you must return to your family property and to your own clan.
11 The fiftieth year will be a Jubilee for you. Do not plant the land. Do not harvest what grows by itself. Do not gather from the vines you did not care for.
12 It is a Jubilee. It must be holy to you. You may eat only the crops taken straight from the field.
13 In this Year of Jubilee, each of you must return to your own property.
14 If you sell something to your neighbor, or buy from him, do not cheat each other.
15 Buy from your neighbor by counting the years since the last Jubilee. He must sell to you by counting the harvest years that are left.
16 If many years remain, raise the price. If only a few years remain, lower the price. He is selling you a number of harvests.
17 Do not cheat each other. Fear your God. I am the LORD your God.
18 Keep my rules and carefully obey my laws. Then you will live safely in the land.
19 The land will give its fruit. You will eat your fill and live in safety in the land.
20 You may ask, 'What will we eat in the seventh year? We did not plant or gather our crops.'
21 I will send my blessing on you in the sixth year. The land will give enough crops for three years.
22 When you plant in the eighth year, you will still eat from the old harvest. You will eat it until the ninth year's harvest comes in.
23 The land must not be sold for ever, because the land is mine. You reside in my land as foreigners and strangers.
24 So for every piece of land you own, you must allow it to be bought back.
25 If your brother Israelite becomes poor and sells some of his property, his closest relative may come and buy back what was sold.
26 A man may have no one to buy it back for him. But if he later has enough money to buy it back,
27 he must count the years since he sold it. He must pay back the difference to the buyer. Then he may return to his property.
28 But if he does not have enough money to repay him, the buyer keeps the land until the Year of Jubilee. In the Jubilee, the land is released. Then he may return to his property.
29 If a man sells a house in a walled city, he may buy it back within one full year of the sale.
30 If he does not buy it back within that year, the house in the walled city belongs to the buyer for ever. It is not released in the Jubilee.
31 But houses in villages without walls are treated like open fields. They may be bought back. They must be released in the Jubilee.
32 As for the cities of the Levites, the Levites always have the right to buy back their houses there.
33 Whatever belongs to the Levites may be redeemed. A house sold in a Levite city must be released in the Jubilee. The houses in the Levite cities are their possession among the Israelites.
34 But the open land around their cities must not be sold. This is their lasting possession.
35 If your fellow Israelite becomes poor and cannot support himself among you, help him. Help him as you would a foreigner or stranger. Then he can keep living among you.
36 Do not take interest or profit from him. Fear your God, so that your fellow Israelite may live among you.
37 You must not lend him money at interest. You must not sell him food for profit.
38 I am the LORD your God. I brought you out of the land of Egypt. I gave you the land of Canaan. I am your God.
39 If a fellow Israelite becomes very poor and sells himself to you, do not make him work as a slave.
40 Let him stay with you as a hired worker or temporary guest. He must work for you until the Year of Jubilee.
41 Then he and his children must be released. He may return to his clan and to the property of his fathers.
42 For the Israelites belong to me as servants. I brought them out of the land of Egypt. They must not be sold as slaves.
43 Do not treat them cruelly. Fear your God.
44 Your male and female servants may come from the nations around you. You may buy them from those nations.
45 You may also buy them from the foreigners who live among you. You may buy from their clans born in your land. These may become your property.
46 You may leave them to your sons after you as property. You can keep them as slaves for life. But you must not treat your brother Israelites cruelly.
47 A foreigner living among you may grow rich. Your fellow Israelite living near him may become very poor. He may sell himself to the foreigner or to a member of his clan.
48 He keeps the right to be bought back after he has sold himself. One of his brothers may redeem him.
49 His uncle, cousin, or any close relative from his clan may redeem him. Or if he grows rich, he may redeem himself.
50 He and his buyer must count the time from the year he sold himself to the Year of Jubilee. The price will be based on the daily wages of a hired worker.
51 If many years remain, he must pay back most of the price.
52 If only a few years remain until the Jubilee, he must count those years and pay for those years.
53 He must be treated like a worker hired by the year. A foreign owner must not treat him cruelly in your sight.
54 Even if he is not redeemed in these ways, he and his children must be released in the Year of Jubilee.
55 For the Israelites belong to me as servants. They are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God."

title: Leviticus 26
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 "Do not make idols for yourselves. Do not set up a carved image or sacred stone pillar. Do not place a carved stone in your land to bow down to it. I am the LORD your God.
2 Keep my Sabbaths. Have respect for my holy place. I am the LORD.
3 If you follow my rules and carefully keep my commands,
4 I will send you rain in the right season. The land will give its crops. The trees in the field will bear their fruit.
5 Your threshing of grain will last until the grape harvest. The grape harvest will last until planting time. You will have plenty of bread to eat. You will live in safety in your land.
6 I will give peace to the land. You will lie down with nothing to fear. I will remove dangerous wild animals from the land. No sword will pass through your land.
7 You will chase your enemies. They will fall by the sword before you.
8 Five of you will chase a hundred. A hundred of you will chase ten thousand. Your enemies will fall by the sword before you.
9 I will look on you with favor. I will make you fruitful and increase your numbers. I will keep my covenant with you.
10 You will still be eating the old grain when you need to clear it out. You will need room for the new harvest.
11 I will put my dwelling place among you. I will not reject you.
12 I will walk among you and be your God. And you will be my people.
13 I am the LORD your God. I brought you out of the land of Egypt. You are no longer slaves to the Egyptians. I broke the bars of your heavy yoke. I helped you walk with your heads held high.
14 But if you do not obey me and do not keep all these commands,
15 if you reject my rules, hate my laws, and refuse to keep all my commands, you will break my covenant.

16 Then this is what I will do to you. I will bring sudden terror on you. I will send wasting disease and fever. They will destroy your sight and drain your life. You will plant your seed for nothing. Your enemies will eat your harvest.
17 I will set my face against you. Your enemies will defeat you. Those who hate you will rule over you. You will run away when no one is chasing you.
18 If after all this you still will not listen to me, I will punish you seven times more for your sins.
19 I will break your proud, stubborn hearts. I will make your sky like iron and your ground like bronze.
20 Your strength will be spent for nothing. Your land will give no crops. The trees of the land will bear no fruit.
21 If you remain against me and refuse to obey me, I will send seven times more troubles, just as your sins deserve.
22 I will send wild animals against you. They will take your children. They will destroy your farm animals. Your people will become few. Your roads will lie empty.
23 If after all this you still will not accept my correction, but continue against me,
24 then I myself will be against you. I will strike you seven times more for your sins.
25 I will bring a sword against you to punish you for breaking the covenant. When you go into your cities, I will send a plague among you. You will fall into the hand of your enemies.
26 I will cut off your supply of bread. Ten women will bake your bread in one oven. They will hand it out by weight. You will eat but will not be full.
27 If in spite of all this you still will not obey me, but continue against me,
28 then I will walk in fury against you. I myself will punish you seven times more for your sins.
29 You will eat the flesh of your own sons and daughters.
30 I will destroy your high places of worship. I will cut down your incense altars. I will pile your dead bodies on the lifeless remains of your idols. I will hate you.
31 I will turn your cities into ruins. I will leave your holy places empty. I will not accept the sweet smell of your sacrifices.
32 I will leave the land in ruins. Even your enemies who live there will be shocked.
33 I will scatter you among the nations. I will draw my sword after you. Your land will become empty. Your cities will lie in ruins.
34 Then the land will enjoy its Sabbath rests. It will rest all the days it lies empty, while you are in the land of your enemies.
35 As long as the land lies empty, it will have the rest. It did not get this rest during the Sabbaths when you lived in it.

36 As for those of you who are left, I will make their hearts so fearful in the lands of their enemies. Even the sound of a leaf blown by the wind will put them to flight. They will run as if they were running from a sword. They will fall when no one is chasing them.
37 They will trip over one another as if before a sword, though no one is behind them. You will not be able to stand against your enemies.
38 You will die among the nations. The land of your enemies will eat you up.
39 Those of you who are left in the lands of your enemies will waste away because of their sins. They will also waste away because of the sins of their fathers.
40 But if they will confess their sins and the sins of their fathers — the unfaithfulness they showed against me, when they walked against me —
41 so that I also walked against them and brought them into the land of their enemies — if their uncircumcised hearts are humbled and they pay for their sin,
42 then I will remember my covenant with Jacob and my covenant with Isaac and my covenant with Abraham. And I will remember the land.
43 The land will be left empty by them. It will enjoy its Sabbath rests while lying empty without them. They will pay for their sin, because they rejected my laws and hated my rules.

44 Yet in spite of this, when they are in the land of their enemies, I will not reject them or hate them so as to destroy them completely. I will not break my covenant with them. I am the LORD their God.
45 For their sake I will remember the covenant with their fathers. I brought them out of the land of Egypt before the eyes of the nations, so that I would be their God. I am the LORD."
46 These are the rules, laws, and commands that the LORD made between himself and the Israelites through Moses on Mount Sinai.

title: Leviticus 27
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Leviticus
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to the Israelites. Tell them: A person may make a special vow to the LORD. The vow may set the value of a person.
3 For a male from twenty to sixty years old, the value is fifty shekels of silver. Use the official shekel.
4 For a female of the same age, the value is thirty shekels.
5 For a male from five to twenty years old, the value is twenty shekels. For a female of the same age, the value is ten shekels.
6 For a male from one month to five years old, the value is five shekels of silver. For a female, the value is three shekels of silver.
7 For a male sixty years or older, the value is fifteen shekels. For a female, the value is ten shekels.
8 If the person is too poor to pay, bring him to the priest. The priest will set the value. He will set it by what the person can afford.
9 A person may vow an animal that can be given to the LORD. Any such animal given to the LORD is holy.
10 He must not replace it or exchange it. He must not trade a good one for a bad one, or a bad one for a good one. If he does swap one animal for another, both animals become holy.
11 A person may vow an unclean animal that cannot be given to the LORD. Then the animal must be brought to the priest.
12 The priest will set its value, whether high or low. The priest's price will stand.
13 If the owner wants to buy the animal back, he must add one fifth to its value.
14 A man may dedicate his house as holy to the LORD. Then the priest will set its value, whether high or low. The priest's price will stand.
15 If he wants to buy his house back, he must add one fifth to the value. Then the house will belong to him again.
16 A man may dedicate to the LORD a piece of his land. Then the value is set by the seed needed for it. Fifty shekels of silver are needed for every homer of barley seed.
17 If he dedicates his field during the Year of Jubilee, the full value will stand.
18 If he dedicates it after the Jubilee, the priest will set the price by the years left. The years run until the next Year of Jubilee. So the value will be less.
19 If he wants to buy the field back, he must add one fifth to the value. Then it will belong to him again.
20 If he does not buy the field back, the field cannot be bought back later. The same is true if he has sold it to another man.
21 When the field is set free in the Jubilee, it becomes holy. It will be like a field devoted to the LORD. It becomes the property of the priests.
22 A man may dedicate to the LORD a field that he has bought. This field is not part of his family land.
23 Then the priest will set its value up to the Year of Jubilee. The man must pay that value on the same day. It is a holy gift to the LORD.
24 In the Year of Jubilee, the field will return to the man who sold it. He is the original owner of the land.
25 Every value must be set in the official shekel. One shekel equals twenty gerahs.
26 But no one may dedicate a firstborn animal. The firstborn already belongs to the LORD. Whether ox or sheep, it is the LORD's.
27 If the firstborn is an unclean animal, the owner may buy it back. He must pay its value and add one fifth. If he does not buy it back, it must be sold at its set value.
28 Nothing that a man devotes to the LORD can be sold or bought back. This is true for any person, animal, or family land he owns. Everything so devoted is most holy to the LORD.
29 No person devoted to destruction can be set free by payment. He must be put to death.
30 A tithe of everything from the land belongs to the LORD. This includes grain from the soil and fruit from the trees. It is holy to the LORD.
31 A man may want to buy back part of his tithe. Then he must add one fifth to its value.
32 Every tenth animal from the herd or flock will be holy to the LORD. They are counted as they pass under the shepherd's rod.
33 The owner must not check whether the animal is good or bad. He must not swap it for another. If he does swap it, both animals become holy. Neither can be bought back."
34 These are the commands the LORD gave Moses at Mount Sinai for the Israelites.
